Coagulation

aPTT in Blood by Coagulation assay32.3 H24 - 32s

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows leukopenia, markedly elevated creatinine, hyperbilirubinemia, mild hyperchloremia, prolonged aPTT, and metabolic acidosis (low pH, normal pCO₂, low-normal bicarbonate). These findings suggest significant renal dysfunction, possible hepatic dysfunction or cholestasis, and increased bleeding risk. Immediate clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.
